I'll provide you guys with an example of what this might look like. Okay. And so I want to show you a quarterly goal that I had. So here's the template that I would like I have included in the modules as an example of something that you guys could work off of. Quarterly goal, create finalized team meetup presentation and perfect verbal delivery. What's my completion date? 129. Department priority. Is this high? Is this low? Is this medium? Right. And because it was a time sensitive one for the end of quarter four, it was high. Resources needed time because creating two to three hour presentations takes a long time. What are some potential roadblocks and delays? I could easily put lack of time here since it takes so much dedicated focus time, but that would be setting in a bad example and I don't want to make excuses. Okay, so I put that as my rope. And so then I broke down what I had to take steps wise to reach my goal. So one was I'm going to use WooFoo feedback from our team to create a presentation outline and goal. So what I did was I created a WooFoo and I said, Hey guys, you know, we have the team meetup coming in, you know, six weeks. I wanted your feedback on what you would like to hear. What would you like me to present on? What would you like to hear from the rest of our team about, right? And I thought, how long will that take to complete? Probably one week because I need to give people full week to get me their feedback and turned in. After that, I said, okay, I'm going to create a roughed outline of topics to cover based on their feedback. And then I'm going to delegate whatever is vision or strategy to Alex. And so why does that take two weeks? It took two weeks because creating the first draft, I like to stop, look over it, stop, look over it, talk about it with Alex, talk about it with Mimi, talk about with some of the directors and get everyone's input, right? Third, I want to reread and create second and third drafts. So I am not the kind of person to do something in one day. I like to make something, think about it, come back to it, make something, think about it, come back to it. So I was like, how long would that take realistically to make, you know, two to three drafts of something that's going to be probably about 500 slides? I was like, probably will take me three weeks because I can probably dedicate and I looked at my calendar. I was like, I have four days I can dedicate to full days of nothing but this, right? After that, hand to graphic designers to add finesse, graphics, colors, pictures to evoke more emotion. And I think what's a fair amount of time I can give them based on how much other stuff is on their plate right now because we have the summit coming up. So they were working on those slides. Thought to myself, okay, about two weeks, right? And lastly, practice and refine the slides and words until the day of speech. How many weeks do I want in advance my slides to be done so that I can purely just practice, right? And for myself, I like to be more than prepared. So I said one to two weeks, it ended up being about two. How will I know I've succeeded? I said when my team decided they were cool and not lame. And something else I could add is that I feel confident in my delivery. I felt my message was communicated and heard by the team. And this is actually something that I completed two weeks ago and it was definitely a success. And so this is just an example, but notice that we have, is it a high priority, medium, a low, right? So which ones are high versus low? I put all high for this because it was time sensitive. How long will it take to complete each of those tasks? And then what is the potential due date? Okay.
